title: The 11 Best Business Password Managers
subtitle: A ranked list of the top password managers for teams, scored on security, admin controls, usability, and integrations.

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Security & Compliance (30%), Admin Controls (25%), User Experience (20%), Integrations (15%), and Pricing/Support (10%).

answer_capsule: The best business password manager is 1Password for its blend of user-friendly design and powerful admin features, followed by Keeper Security for its enterprise-grade compliance and Dashlane for its intuitive user experience.

criteria.name: Security & Compliance
criteria.description: Strength of encryption (AES-256), zero-knowledge architecture, third-party audits (SOC 2), compliance certifications, and security incident history.

criteria.name: Admin Controls & Provisioning
criteria.description: Granularity of role-based access controls (RBAC), policy enforcement, user provisioning (SCIM), SSO integration, and audit logging capabilities.

criteria.name: User Experience & Adoption
criteria.description: Ease of use for non-technical employees, quality of browser extensions and mobile apps, and reliability of autofill functionality.

criteria.name: Integration & Ecosystem
criteria.description: Availability of integrations with IdPs (Okta, Azure AD), SIEMs, developer tools (CLI), and other core business systems.

criteria.name: Pricing & Support
criteria.description: Transparency and value of pricing tiers, quality of customer support, and availability of onboarding resources.

query_intents: secure way to share passwords with team

solves: Driving user adoption across the company

how_to_choose: Assess your primary need: Is it simple password sharing for a small team, or enterprise-wide credential management with strict compliance rules?
Evaluate the user experience: A tool is only effective if your team actually uses it. Test browser extensions and mobile apps with a pilot group.
Check for key integrations: Ensure the manager integrates with your existing Identity Provider (like Okta or Azure AD) for seamless provisioning and SSO.
Review security architecture: Prioritize providers with a zero-knowledge model, strong encryption (AES-256), and regular third-party security audits (e.g., SOC 2 Type 2).

faqs.q: Why does my business need a password manager?
faqs.a: A business password manager eliminates risky practices like sharing passwords in spreadsheets or chat messages. It centralizes credential management, enforces strong password policies, simplifies employee onboarding/offboarding, and provides an audit trail for compliance.

faqs.q: What's the difference between a business and a personal password manager?
faqs.a: Business plans include critical administrative features not found in personal versions, such as centralized user management, role-based access controls, shared vaults for teams, security policies, advanced reporting, and integration with single sign-on (SSO) systems.

faqs.q: How secure are business password managers?
faqs.a: Leading providers use a 'zero-knowledge' architecture, meaning they never have access to your unencrypted data. Your information is encrypted and decrypted locally on your device using your master password as the key. Look for providers with strong encryption (AES-256) and regular third-party security audits like SOC 2.

SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) is a protocol that automates user provisioning. When integrated with your Identity Provider (like Azure AD or Okta), it automatically creates, updates, and deactivates user accounts in the password manager as employees join, change roles, or leave the company.

honest_disclosures: This list prioritizes SaaS solutions with strong adoption among non-technical teams. More complex, self-hosted, or developer-centric tools like Passbolt are included but ranked lower.
We acknowledge the past security incidents at LastPass, which has impacted its 'Security & Compliance' score in our methodology.

glossary.definition: A security model where the service provider has zero knowledge of the data stored on their servers. Data is encrypted and decrypted client-side (on the user's device), so the provider cannot access the unencrypted contents.

entries.name: 1Password
entries.best_for: Teams seeking the best overall balance of exceptional user experience, robust security, and developer-friendly features.
entries.verdict: 1Password earns the top rank for its unmatched combination of an intuitive, best-in-class user interface that drives adoption and powerful, developer-centric features like Secrets Automation.
entries.verdict_short: Unmatched blend of usability, security, and developer tools makes it the top choice for most businesses.
entries.praise: Its browser extension is widely considered the most reliable and user-friendly, and its 'Watchtower' feature provides excellent security insights.
entries.praise_short: Best-in-class browser extension and security insights.
entries.criticism: The pricing is at the premium end of the market, which can be a consideration for very large teams or budget-conscious startups.
entries.criticism_short: Premium pricing compared to some competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Keeper Security
entries.best_for: Organizations in regulated industries that require top-tier security, extensive compliance certifications, and granular administrative controls.
entries.verdict: Keeper secures the second spot due to its unparalleled focus on security and compliance, holding certifications like FedRAMP authorization that are critical for government and enterprise clients.
entries.verdict_short: The leader in security certifications and granular admin controls, ideal for regulated industries.
entries.praise: Its admin console offers incredibly detailed role-based policies and enforcement settings, along with a robust add-on ecosystem for secrets management and connection management.
entries.praise_short: Extremely granular admin controls and strong compliance.
entries.criticism: The user interface, while powerful, can feel slightly less modern and intuitive for end-users compared to 1Password or Dashlane.
entries.criticism_short: User interface is less polished than top rivals.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Dashlane
entries.best_for: Teams that prioritize a simple, clean user experience to maximize employee adoption and password health.
entries.verdict: Dashlane ranks third by offering one of the most intuitive and user-friendly interfaces in the market, making it exceptionally easy to deploy and drive adoption across an entire organization.
entries.verdict_short: Excellent user interface and features like a VPN make it a strong choice for driving adoption.
entries.praise: Features like one-click password changing and a clear password health dashboard are highly effective at encouraging better security habits among employees.
entries.praise_short: Simple UI and effective password health tools.
entries.criticism: Its integrations and advanced administrative controls are not as extensive as those offered by 1Password or Keeper, making it better suited for SMBs than complex enterprises.
entries.criticism_short: Fewer advanced admin controls and integrations.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Bitwarden
entries.best_for: Businesses looking for a powerful, open-source, and cost-effective solution, with the option for self-hosting.
entries.verdict: Bitwarden is the highest-ranking open-source option, valued for its transparency, robust security model, and highly competitive pricing, making it the best value on the market.
entries.verdict_short: The best value, open-source password manager, offering transparency and a self-hosting option.
entries.praise: Its open-source nature allows for public code audits, providing a high level of trust, and its pricing is significantly lower than most competitors.
entries.praise_short: Transparent open-source code and unbeatable pricing.
entries.criticism: The user interface and overall user experience are more functional than polished, lagging behind the slick designs of 1Password and Dashlane.
entries.criticism_short: User interface is less refined than premium rivals.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: LastPass
entries.best_for: Companies looking for a mature, feature-rich platform with a long track record and extensive integrations.
entries.verdict: LastPass remains a top contender due to its mature feature set, comprehensive admin dashboard, and wide adoption, though its ranking is impacted by a lower security score following past incidents.
entries.verdict_short: A feature-rich and widely adopted platform, but its score is tempered by recent security history.
entries.praise: The platform offers a very comprehensive set of administrative controls and policies, along with one of the broadest sets of integrations available.
entries.praise_short: Comprehensive admin controls and broad integrations.
entries.criticism: Recent, well-publicized security breaches have damaged trust and raised concerns about its security architecture and incident response.
entries.criticism_short: Past security breaches have impacted trust.

risk_signals.summary: Has experienced significant public security breaches in the past (2022), which impacted customer vault data. The company has since published remediation efforts.

entries.name: Delinea
entries.best_for: Large enterprises needing a comprehensive Privileged Access Management (PAM) solution that includes robust password vaulting.
entries.verdict: Delinea (formerly ThycoticCentrify) is ranked for its enterprise-grade Privileged Access Management (PAM) capabilities, offering far more than just password management, including session recording and server access controls.
entries.verdict_short: A powerful, enterprise-focused PAM solution that goes far beyond standard password management.
entries.praise: It provides exceptional control over privileged accounts, which is critical for securing infrastructure and meeting stringent compliance requirements in large organizations.
entries.praise_short: Excellent control over privileged IT accounts.
entries.criticism: The platform is complex and expensive, making it overkill for teams that only need basic password sharing and vaulting.
entries.criticism_short: Complex and expensive; overkill for simple needs.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: NordPass
entries.best_for: Small to medium-sized businesses that value simplicity, a modern interface, and next-generation encryption.
entries.verdict: NordPass, from the makers of NordVPN, earns its place by focusing on a streamlined user experience and strong, modern encryption (XChaCha20), making it a great choice for less complex business needs.
entries.verdict_short: A simple, modern manager with next-gen encryption, ideal for SMBs prioritizing ease of use.
entries.praise: Its use of the XChaCha20 encryption algorithm is a forward-looking choice, and its interface is clean and uncluttered.
entries.praise_short: Modern XChaCha20 encryption and clean UI.
entries.criticism: The platform lacks the advanced administrative controls, reporting, and deep integrations (like SCIM) required by larger enterprises.
entries.criticism_short: Lacks advanced enterprise features and integrations.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: CyberArk
entries.best_for: Global enterprises with the most demanding security and compliance needs for privileged accounts and secrets.
entries.verdict: CyberArk is a leader in the Privileged Access Management (PAM) space, offering an incredibly powerful but complex solution designed to secure the most critical assets in large, global organizations.
entries.verdict_short: The market leader in enterprise-grade PAM, built for the most complex security environments.
entries.praise: The platform's ability to discover, manage, and audit every type of privileged credential across a hybrid enterprise is unmatched.
entries.praise_short: Unmatched control over all privileged credentials.
entries.criticism: CyberArk is notoriously complex to implement and manage, and its cost puts it out of reach for all but the largest enterprises.
entries.criticism_short: Extremely complex, high cost, and steep learning curve.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: RoboForm for Business
entries.best_for: Small businesses that need reliable password management and best-in-class web form filling capabilities.
entries.verdict: RoboForm, a veteran in the space, is ranked for its industry-leading form-filling capabilities, which remain a key differentiator, although its business admin features are less developed than market leaders.
entries.verdict_short: A long-standing provider with exceptional form-filling, best for SMBs with simpler needs.
entries.praise: Its ability to accurately capture and fill complex, multi-page web forms is still among the best available, saving significant time for users.
entries.criticism: The admin console and policy controls are relatively basic, and it lacks advanced integrations like SCIM provisioning.
entries.criticism_short: Basic admin controls and limited integrations.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Zoho Vault
entries.best_for: Teams already heavily invested in the Zoho ecosystem of business applications.
entries.verdict: Zoho Vault makes the list as a solid, cost-effective option for businesses that are already using Zoho's suite of products, thanks to its seamless integration with tools like Zoho CRM and Projects.
entries.verdict_short: A solid and affordable choice, especially for companies already using other Zoho products.
entries.praise: The deep integration with the broader Zoho One suite provides a unified admin and user experience for existing Zoho customers.
entries.praise_short: Tight integration with Zoho One suite.
entries.criticism: As a standalone product, it lacks the polished user experience and advanced feature set to compete directly with the top-tier dedicated password managers.
entries.criticism_short: Less competitive as a standalone product.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.

entries.name: Passbolt
entries.best_for: Development and DevOps teams who need an open-source, self-hostable password manager designed for collaboration.
entries.verdict: Passbolt is our wildcard pick because it's built from the ground up for a specific audience—technical teams—with an open-source, GPG-based security model that prioritizes developer workflows and self-hosting.
entries.verdict_short: A unique, open-source, self-hostable option built specifically for developers and DevOps teams.
entries.praise: Its security model is based on OpenPGP, a battle-tested standard, and its focus on collaboration within technical teams is a unique and valuable approach.
entries.praise_short: Strong GPG-based security and developer focus.
entries.criticism: It requires significant technical expertise to set up and maintain (if self-hosted), and its user experience is not designed for non-technical business users.
entries.criticism_short: Requires technical skill; not for non-devs.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-10.
